title: Getting Started with rurality
text: rurality package provides rurality classification data U.S. counties ZIP codes. bundles USDA Rural-Urban Continuum Codes (RUCC 2023), Rural-Urban Commuting Area codes (RUCA 2020), composite rurality score combines multiple data sources single 0–100 measure. package designed researchers need classify locations rurality without manually downloading reshaping USDA spreadsheets.
code: library(rurality) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

title: Getting Started with rurality
text: simplest use case looking rurality data county 5-digit FIPS code: just need score RUCC code: Multiple FIPS codes work :
code: get_rurality("05031") #> # A tibble: 1 × 24 #>   fips  state_fips county_fips state_abbr county_name      pop_2020 acs_pop #>   <chr> <chr>      <chr>       <chr>      <chr>               <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 05031 05         031         AR         Craighead County   111231  111038 #> # ℹ 17 more variables: land_area_sqmi <dbl>, pop_density <dbl>, #> #   rucc_2023 <int>, rucc_description <chr>, omb_designation <chr>, lat <dbl>, #> #   lng <dbl>, dist_large_metro <dbl>, dist_medium_metro <dbl>, #> #   dist_small_metro <dbl>, rucc_score <dbl>, density_score <dbl>, #> #   distance_score <dbl>, rurality_score <dbl>, rurality_classification <chr>, #> #   median_income <dbl>, median_age <dbl> rurality_score("05031") #>  3  #> 40 get_rucc("05031") #> [1] 3 rurality_score(c("05031", "06037", "48453")) #>  3  1  1  #> 40 17 23

title: Getting Started with rurality
text: RUCA codes available ZIP/ZCTA level:
code: get_ruca("72401") #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   zip   state primary_ruca secondary_ruca #>   <chr> <chr>        <int>          <int> #> 1 72401 AR               1              1 get_ruca(c("72401", "90210", "59801")) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   zip   state primary_ruca secondary_ruca #>   <chr> <chr>        <int>          <int> #> 1 59801 MT               1              1 #> 2 72401 AR               1              1 #> 3 90210 CA               1              1

title: Getting Started with rurality
text: common research workflow merging rurality data onto existing dataset. add_rurality() function handles : default, three columns added: rurality_score, rurality_classification, rucc_2023. Use vars = "" full set: FIPS column different name, specify :
code: my_data <- data.frame(   fips = c("05031", "06037", "48453", "30063"),   outcome = c(0.72, 0.41, 0.58, 0.89) )  my_data |> add_rurality() #>    fips outcome rurality_score rurality_classification rucc_2023 #> 1 05031    0.72             40                   Mixed         3 #> 2 06037    0.41             17                   Urban         1 #> 3 48453    0.58             23                Suburban         1 #> 4 30063    0.89             45                   Mixed         3 my_data |> add_rurality(vars = "all") |> glimpse() #> Rows: 4 #> Columns: 25 #> $ fips                    <chr> "05031", "06037", "48453", "30063" #> $ outcome                 <dbl> 0.72, 0.41, 0.58, 0.89 #> $ state_fips              <chr> "05", "06", "48", "30" #> $ county_fips             <chr> "031", "037", "453", "063" #> $ state_abbr              <chr> "AR", "CA", "TX", "MT" #> $ county_name             <chr> "Craighead County", "Los Angeles County", "Tra… #> $ pop_2020                <dbl> 111231, 10014009, 1290188, 117922 #> $ acs_pop                 <dbl> 111038, 9936690, 1289054, 118541 #> $ land_area_sqmi          <dbl> 707.1583, 4059.2816, 994.0526, 2593.0085 #> $ pop_density             <dbl> 157.0, 2447.9, 1296.8, 45.7 #> $ rucc_2023               <int> 3, 1, 1, 3 #> $ rucc_description        <chr> "Metro - Counties in metro areas of fewer than… #> $ omb_designation         <chr> "Metropolitan", "Metropolitan", "Metropolitan"… #> $ lat                     <dbl> 35.83091, 34.32080, 30.33436, 47.03601 #> $ lng                     <dbl> -90.63290, -118.22485, -97.78182, -113.92470 #> $ dist_large_metro        <dbl> 382.3440, 18.5902, 149.6074, 395.5553 #> $ dist_medium_metro       <dbl> 119.7631, 188.6871, 441.7468, 169.4502 #> $ dist_small_metro        <dbl> 4.076085, 377.962597, 558.870426, 11.787297 #> $ rucc_score              <dbl> 28, 8, 8, 28 #> $ density_score           <dbl> 45, 15, 22, 59 #> $ distance_score          <dbl> 69, 51, 75, 78 #> $ rurality_score          <dbl> 40, 17, 23, 45 #> $ rurality_classification <chr> "Mixed", "Urban", "Suburban", "Mixed" #> $ median_income           <dbl> 55169, 83411, 92731, 66840 #> $ median_age              <dbl> 34.4, 37.4, 35.1, 36.7 other_data <- data.frame(county_fips = c("05031", "06037"), y = 1:2) other_data |> add_rurality(fips_col = "county_fips") #>   county_fips y rurality_score rurality_classification rucc_2023 #> 1       05031 1             40                   Mixed         3 #> 2       06037 2             17                   Urban         1

title: Getting Started with rurality
text: classify_rurality() function converts numeric scores labels: thresholds :
code: classify_rurality(c(10, 30, 50, 70, 90)) #> [1] "Urban"      "Suburban"   "Mixed"      "Rural"      "Very Rural"

title: Getting Started with rurality
text: county_rurality dataset contains 3,235 U.S. counties: Filter state:
code: county_rurality #> # A tibble: 3,235 × 24 #>    fips  state_fips county_fips state_abbr county_name     pop_2020 acs_pop #>    <chr> <chr>      <chr>       <chr>      <chr>              <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 01001 01         001         AL         Autauga County     58805   58761 #>  2 01003 01         003         AL         Baldwin County    231767  233420 #>  3 01005 01         005         AL         Barbour County     25223   24877 #>  4 01007 01         007         AL         Bibb County        22293   22251 #>  5 01009 01         009         AL         Blount County      59134   59077 #>  6 01011 01         011         AL         Bullock County     10357   10328 #>  7 01013 01         013         AL         Butler County      19051   18981 #>  8 01015 01         015         AL         Calhoun County    116441  116162 #>  9 01017 01         017         AL         Chambers County    34772   34612 #> 10 01019 01         019         AL         Cherokee County    24971   25069 #> # ℹ 3,225 more rows #> # ℹ 17 more variables: land_area_sqmi <dbl>, pop_density <dbl>, #> #   rucc_2023 <int>, rucc_description <chr>, omb_designation <chr>, lat <dbl>, #> #   lng <dbl>, dist_large_metro <dbl>, dist_medium_metro <dbl>, #> #   dist_small_metro <dbl>, rucc_score <dbl>, density_score <dbl>, #> #   distance_score <dbl>, rurality_score <dbl>, rurality_classification <chr>, #> #   median_income <dbl>, median_age <dbl> county_rurality |>   filter(state_abbr == "AR") |>   select(county_name, rurality_score, rurality_classification, rucc_2023) |>   arrange(desc(rurality_score)) |>   head(10) #> # A tibble: 10 × 4 #>    county_name    rurality_score rurality_classification rucc_2023 #>    <chr>                   <dbl> <chr>                       <int> #>  1 Calhoun County             85 Very Rural                      9 #>  2 Chicot County              85 Very Rural                      9 #>  3 Newton County              85 Very Rural                      9 #>  4 Searcy County              85 Very Rural                      9 #>  5 Desha County               84 Very Rural                      9 #>  6 Fulton County              84 Very Rural                      9 #>  7 Monroe County              84 Very Rural                      9 #>  8 Clay County                83 Very Rural                      9 #>  9 Lincoln County             83 Very Rural                      9 #> 10 Marion County              83 Very Rural                      9

title: Getting Started with rurality
code: if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   ggplot2::ggplot(county_rurality, ggplot2::aes(x = rurality_score)) +     ggplot2::geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "#15803d", color = "white") +     ggplot2::labs(       title = "Distribution of Rurality Scores Across U.S. Counties",       x = "Rurality Score (0-100)",       y = "Number of Counties"     ) +     ggplot2::theme_minimal() } #> Warning: Removed 22 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range #> (`stat_bin()`).

title: Getting Started with rurality
text: composite rurality score weighted average three components: full details, see rurality.app.

title: Getting Started with rurality

R package version 0.1.0, #>   <https://rurality.app>. #>  #> A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is #>  #>   @Manual{, #>     title = {rurality: Rurality Classification and Scoring for U.S. Counties and ZIP Codes}, #>     author = {Cameron Wimpy}, #>     year = {2026}, #>     url = {https://rurality.app}, #>     note = {R package version 0.1.0}, #>   }

what: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: Documentation: https://cwimpy.github.io/rurality/ Rurality classification scoring U.S. counties ZIP codes. Provides USDA Rural-Urban Continuum Codes (RUCC 2023), Rural-Urban Commuting Area codes (RUCA 2020), composite rurality score 3,235 U.S. counties. Built make rurality data easy use research without manually downloading merging USDA spreadsheets. Web app: rurality.app

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("rurality") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("cwimpy/rurality")

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
code: library(rurality)  # Look up a county by FIPS get_rurality("05031") #> Craighead County, AR — Score: 40 (Mixed), RUCC: 3  # Just the score rurality_score("05031") #> 40  # Just the RUCC code get_rucc("05031") #> 3  # RUCA code for a ZIP get_ruca("72401") #> Primary RUCA: 1 (Metropolitan core)  # Merge onto your own data my_data <- data.frame(   fips = c("05031", "06037", "48453"),   outcome = c(0.7, 0.4, 0.6) ) my_data |> add_rurality()  # Add all available variables my_data |> add_rurality(vars = "all")

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: package ships two datasets:

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: 3,235 U.S. counties 24 variables including:
code: # Browse the full dataset county_rurality  # Filter to a state county_rurality |> dplyr::filter(state_abbr == "AR")  # Distribution table(county_rurality$rurality_classification) #> Mixed    Rural Suburban    Urban Very Rural #>   663      921      781       87        783

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: USDA RUCA codes (2020) 41,146 ZIP code tabulation areas.
code: ruca_codes |> dplyr::filter(state == "AR")

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: composite rurality score weighted average three components: Scores range 0 (urban) 100 (rural). See full methodology details.

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: use package published research, please cite:
code: Wimpy, Cameron (2026). rurality: Rurality Classification and Scoring   for U.S. Counties and ZIP Codes. R package version 0.1.0.   https://github.com/cwimpy/rurality

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: USDA ERS Rural-Urban Continuum Codes, 2023 USDA ERS Rural-Urban Commuting Area Codes, 2020 U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey 2022 5-Year Estimates U.S. Census Bureau, TIGER/Line Shapefiles 2020

title: Classification and Scoring of U.S. County and ZIP Code Rurality
text: MIT

what: Merge Rurality Data onto a Data Frame — add_rurality
title: Merge Rurality Data onto a Data Frame — add_rurality
text: Joins rurality data onto existing data frame FIPS code.

title: Merge Rurality Data onto a Data Frame — add_rurality
code: add_rurality(   data,   fips_col = "fips",   vars = c("rurality_score", "rurality_classification", "rucc_2023") )

title: Merge Rurality Data onto a Data Frame — add_rurality
text: data data frame FIPS code column. fips_col name FIPS code column (default: "fips"). vars rurality variables add. Default adds score classification. Use "" variables.

title: Merge Rurality Data onto a Data Frame — add_rurality
text: input data frame rurality columns appended.

title: Merge Rurality Data onto a Data Frame — add_rurality
code: my_data <- data.frame(fips = c("05031", "06037", "48453"), value = 1:3) add_rurality(my_data) #>    fips value rurality_score rurality_classification rucc_2023 #> 1 05031     1             40                   Mixed         3 #> 2 06037     2             17                   Urban         1 #> 3 48453     3             23                Suburban         1 add_rurality(my_data, vars = "all") #>    fips value state_fips county_fips state_abbr        county_name pop_2020 #> 1 05031     1         05         031         AR   Craighead County   111231 #> 2 06037     2         06         037         CA Los Angeles County 10014009 #> 3 48453     3         48         453         TX      Travis County  1290188 #>   acs_pop land_area_sqmi pop_density rucc_2023 #> 1  111038       707.1583       157.0         3 #> 2 9936690      4059.2816      2447.9         1 #> 3 1289054       994.0526      1296.8         1 #>                                        rucc_description omb_designation #> 1 Metro - Counties in metro areas of fewer than 250,000    Metropolitan #> 2         Metro - Counties in metro areas of 1 million+    Metropolitan #> 3         Metro - Counties in metro areas of 1 million+    Metropolitan #>        lat        lng dist_large_metro dist_medium_metro dist_small_metro #> 1 35.83091  -90.63290         382.3440          119.7631         4.076085 #> 2 34.32080 -118.22485          18.5902          188.6871       377.962597 #> 3 30.33436  -97.78182         149.6074          441.7468       558.870426 #>   rucc_score density_score distance_score rurality_score #> 1         28            45             69             40 #> 2          8            15             51             17 #> 3          8            22             75             23 #>   rurality_classification median_income median_age #> 1                   Mixed         55169       34.4 #> 2                   Urban         83411       37.4 #> 3                Suburban         92731       35.1

title: Classify a Rurality Score — classify_rurality
text: Converts numeric rurality scores classification labels.

title: Classify a Rurality Score — classify_rurality
code: classify_rurality(score)

title: Classify a Rurality Score — classify_rurality
text: score numeric vector rurality scores (0-100).

title: Classify a Rurality Score — classify_rurality
text: character vector classifications.

title: Classify a Rurality Score — classify_rurality
code: classify_rurality(c(15, 35, 55, 75, 90)) #> [1] "Urban"      "Suburban"   "Mixed"      "Rural"      "Very Rural"

what: County-Level Rurality Data for the United States — county_rurality
title: County-Level Rurality Data for the United States — county_rurality
text: dataset containing rurality scores, USDA classifications, demographic data U.S. counties. Includes RUCC 2023 codes, population density, distance metro areas, composite rurality score.

title: County-Level Rurality Data for the United States — county_rurality
code: county_rurality

title: County-Level Rurality Data for the United States — county_rurality
text: tibble approximately 3,233 rows 23 columns: fips 5-digit county FIPS code (character) state_fips 2-digit state FIPS code (character) county_fips 3-digit county FIPS code (character) state_abbr Two-letter state abbreviation county_name County name pop_2020 2020 Census population acs_pop ACS 2022 5-year population estimate land_area_sqmi Land area square miles pop_density Population per square mile rucc_2023 USDA Rural-Urban Continuum Code (1-9) rucc_description RUCC code description omb_designation OMB designation: Metropolitan, Micropolitan, Nonmetro lat County centroid latitude lng County centroid longitude dist_large_metro Distance nearest large metro (>1M pop) miles dist_medium_metro Distance nearest medium metro (250K-1M) miles dist_small_metro Distance nearest small metro (50K-250K) miles rucc_score RUCC-derived score component (0-100) density_score Population density score component (0-100) distance_score Distance metro score component (0-100) rurality_score Composite rurality score (0-100) rurality_classification Classification: Urban, Suburban, Mixed, Rural, Rural median_income ACS 2022 median household income median_age ACS 2022 median age

title: County-Level Rurality Data for the United States — county_rurality
text: USDA Economic Research Service, Rural-Urban Continuum Codes 2023 U.S. Census Bureau, American Community Survey 2022 5-Year Estimates U.S. Census Bureau, TIGER/Line Shapefiles 2020

title: County-Level Rurality Data for the United States — county_rurality
text: composite rurality score calculated weighted average: RUCC score: 55\ Population density score: 28\ Distance metro score: 17\ Classifications: 80-100: Rural 60-79: Rural 40-59: Mixed 20-39: Suburban 0-19: Urban

title: County-Level Rurality Data for the United States — county_rurality

what: Look Up RUCA Code by ZIP Code — get_ruca
title: Look Up RUCA Code by ZIP Code — get_ruca
text: Returns USDA Rural-Urban Commuting Area code (2020) one ZIP codes ZCTAs.

title: Look Up RUCA Code by ZIP Code — get_ruca
code: get_ruca(zip)

title: Look Up RUCA Code by ZIP Code — get_ruca
text: zip character vector 5-digit ZIP codes.

title: Look Up RUCA Code by ZIP Code — get_ruca
text: tibble columns: zip, primary_ruca, secondary_ruca, state. Returns NA values ZIPs RUCA dataset.

title: Look Up RUCA Code by ZIP Code — get_ruca
text: RUCA codes range 1 (metropolitan core) 10 (rural). primary code reflects majority commuting pattern; secondary code captures additional commuting flows.

title: Look Up RUCA Code by ZIP Code — get_ruca
code: get_ruca("72401") #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   zip   state primary_ruca secondary_ruca #>   <chr> <chr>        <int>          <int> #> 1 72401 AR               1              1 get_ruca(c("72401", "90210", "59801")) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   zip   state primary_ruca secondary_ruca #>   <chr> <chr>        <int>          <int> #> 1 59801 MT               1              1 #> 2 72401 AR               1              1 #> 3 90210 CA               1              1

what: Get RUCC Code for a County — get_rucc
title: Get RUCC Code for a County — get_rucc
text: Returns USDA Rural-Urban Continuum Code (2023) one counties.

title: Get RUCC Code for a County — get_rucc
code: get_rucc(fips)

title: Get RUCC Code for a County — get_rucc
text: fips character vector 5-digit county FIPS codes.

title: Get RUCC Code for a County — get_rucc
text: integer vector RUCC codes (1-9), NA unmatched FIPS.

title: Get RUCC Code for a County — get_rucc

what: Look Up Rurality Data by FIPS Code — get_rurality
title: Look Up Rurality Data by FIPS Code — get_rurality
text: Returns full rurality record one county FIPS codes.

title: Look Up Rurality Data by FIPS Code — get_rurality
code: get_rurality(fips)

title: Look Up Rurality Data by FIPS Code — get_rurality
text: fips character vector 5-digit county FIPS codes.

title: Look Up Rurality Data by FIPS Code — get_rurality
text: tibble rurality data matched counties.

title: Look Up Rurality Data by FIPS Code — get_rurality

rurality 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-10 Initial CRAN release (2026-04-10).

title: rurality 0.1.0
text: get_rurality() returns composite rurality score, classification, RUCC code U.S. county identified 5-digit FIPS. get_rucc() rurality_score() return respective scalar values one-lookups. get_ruca() returns primary RUCA code ZIP code tabulation area. classify_rurality() converts numeric rurality score (0-100) ordered classification: Urban, Suburban, Mixed, Rural, Rural. add_rurality() joins rurality variables onto existing data frame FIPS column, optional selection full variable set.

title: rurality 0.1.0
text: county_rurality: 3,235 U.S. counties 24 variables, including composite score, classification, RUCC 2023, population density, distance nearest large metro, ACS 2022 demographics. ruca_codes: USDA RUCA 2020 codes 41,146 ZIP code tabulation areas.

title: rurality 0.1.0
text: Composite score weighted average RUCC code (55%), population density (28%), distance nearest large metro (17%), rescaled 0-100.
